cyst_kidney: A well-circumscribed, round hypodense lesion with thin walls and homogeneous fluid attenuation, but arising from the adrenal gland, mimicking a renal cyst’s imaging appearance despite distinct anatomical origin.

A multilocular cystic structure with fine septations and mild peripheral enhancement, resembling a complex kidney cyst but representing a cystic variant of renal cell carcinoma with neoplastic growth patterns.
A hypoattenuating lesion with air pockets and thick, irregular walls on CT, visually similar to a hemorrhagic cyst but indicative of a suppurative renal abscess with acute inflammatory changes.
A dilated, fluid-filled calyx connected to the renal pelvis, displaying cyst-like features but secondary to obstructive uropathy with associated hydronephrosis and thinning renal parenchyma.
A post-traumatic perirenal fluid collection with layered hyperdense material on CT, simulating a hemorrhagic cyst but reflecting a hematoma with clot sedimentation and history of renal injury.
kidney_stone: A smooth, round calcification in the renal pelvis resembling a phlebolith, with homogeneous density but lacking the irregular margins of a true kidney stone.
Linear, branching calcifications along renal vasculature mimicking stone clusters, caused by vascular calcification rather than crystalline aggregation.
Hyperdense, oval structures in the ureteric region with a laminated texture, mistaken for stones but representing retained contrast material or blood clots.
Focal areas of increased density near the renal cortex with a "blooming" artifact on CT, resembling stones but caused by metal streaking from surgical clips.
Small, rounded calcifications in the bladder base with irregular borders, simulating distal ureteral stones but representing benign cystoliths or enteric content overlap.
kidney_tumor: A hyperechoic renal cyst with internal septations and peripheral calcifications mimicking solid tumor vascularity on imaging.
A densely calcified renal stone surrounded by perinephric edema resembling tumor necrosis and inflammatory margins.
A focal pyelonephritic abscess with central necrosis and irregular enhancing borders mistaken for malignant mass infiltration.
A benign oncocytoma with heterogeneous contrast uptake and a central fibrous scar mimicking tumor necrosis and calcification.
A post-traumatic renal hematoma with layered blood products and irregular margins simulating tumor heterogeneity and invasion.
normal_kidney: A kidney with a small, isoattenuating renal mass lacking necrosis or calcification, mimicking normal parenchymal density and texture on cross-sectional imaging.
A horseshoe kidney with a central isthmus connecting both poles, creating a smooth, continuous contour that resembles a single normal kidney.
A simple renal cyst with imperceptibly thin walls and homogeneous fluid density, indistinguishable from adjacent normal renal tissue in attenuation and shape.
Early obstructive hydronephrosis with mild caliectasis but preserved cortical thickness, visually overlapping with normal renal morphology due to subtle structural changes.
A kidney affected by diffuse lipid infiltration, producing a uniformly decreased density similar to normal parenchyma without focal lesions or architectural distortion.
